member.summary:
Resolving the factory is now being executed asynchronously. Because this may trigger a host process reload due to package installations, it cannot be awaited.
Instead, the result will be sent using the platform specific designer communication channel (ex: on Windows this will be IDesignerMessagingService)

member.name: T:UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.ErrorConstants
member.summary: Constants used across Studio Activities API for error handling and exception metadata.

remarks.para: These constants define standard keys used in exception Data dictionaries to provide structured error information to API consumers.
API methods that throw exceptions include error codes in the exception's Data dictionary using the keys defined in this class. Consumers can use these keys to retrieve error information:

This class is designed to be extended as new API services are added to the Studio Activities API. New constants can be added here to define additional metadata keys for exception handling (e.g., validation details, context information, suggested actions).

item.description: : Used by to provide error codes in exceptions

member.name: F:UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.ErrorConstants.ErrorCode
member.summary: Key used in exception Data dictionaries to store error codes.

remarks.para: When API methods throw exceptions, they include a specific error code in the exception's Data dictionary using this key. The error code is a string constant that uniquely identifies the failure reason, enabling consumers to programmatically handle specific error scenarios.

Each API service defines its own set of error codes as string constants. For example, defines error codes specific to the ArgumentService API. Future services will define their own error code classes.

item.description: Enables precise error handling without parsing exception messages
Provides stable error identifiers across localized exception messages
Allows API consumers to handle specific failures programmatically
Supports telemetry and logging with structured error classification

member.name: T:UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.Expressions.IDesignTimeExpressionExpanderService
member.summary: Service used to expand/unwrap dynamic expressions at design time, for e.g. dynamic file paths.

member.summary: Expands the given string expression.

member.name: M:UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.Expressions.IDesignTimeExpressionExpanderService.GenerateDynamicString(System.String)
member.summary: Generates a dynamic expression string based on the specified value. For example, if the given string is a file path that that is user-specific (e.g. a file path that points to something in My Documents), then this will turn that static string into a dynamic expression that can be "expanded" by at design time and evaluated by WWF at runtime, and that is user agnostic. It turns "C:\Users\SomeUser\SomeFolder" to something like string.Format("{}\SomeFolder", Environment.UserFolder).

member.name: T:UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.IRegisterWorkflowDesignApi
member.summary:
Workflow designer API registration. It follows the same pattern as IRegisterMetadata. Studio will scan the types in your assembly for implementations of this interface.
If an implementation is found, an instance is created by Studio and the API instance is handed through .
Note that older Studio versions that do not ship with the API will not be able to load your assembly
If you want to keep the compatibility with the older Studio versions, instead of implementing this interface,
add an Initialize(object) method on the implementation of IRegisterMetadata, that Studio will discover through reflection. Cast the object parameter to on your side
The same instance may be retrieved from the designer context through GetService

member.name: T:UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.Locations.IArgumentService
member.summary: Service to manage arguments in the workflow.

remarks.para: For WPF environments, all API methods must be called from the Dispatcher thread.
Methods throw for input validation failures (invalid names, wrong direction, etc.) and for operational failures (no designer available, argument not found, etc.).
All exceptions include an error code in the Data dictionary (key: ErrorConstants.ErrorCode). Error codes are defined in .

member.name: M:UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.Locations.IArgumentService.GetArguments(System.Func{System.Type,System.Boolean})
member.summary: Get the list of available arguments in the active workflow designer.
member.param: Function used to filter the arguments we get back based on the type of the argument
member.returns: The list of arguments filtered

member.name: M:UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.Locations.IArgumentService.GetArguments(UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.Locations.GetArgumentsArgs)
member.summary: Get the list of available arguments in the context described by the given arguments.

member.name: M:UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.Locations.IArgumentService.SetDefaultValue(UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.Locations.ArgumentInfo,System.String)
member.summary: Sets the default value for an In argument.
member.param: The argument to have the default value changed. Must be an In argument.

Expression string in VB or C# syntax (depending on project language).
String literals must be quoted: Use "\"hello\"" for string values.
Literal detection: Simple literal values (e.g., "42", "\"text\"", "True") are automatically converted to typed literals. Complex expressions (e.g., "DateTime.Now", "variableName") are kept as VB/C# expressions.
member.exception:
Thrown when:
The argument is not an In argument (error code: OnlyInArgumentsSupported)

Thrown when:
Unable to resolve the designer (error code: NoActiveDesigner)
Argument with the given name does not exist (error code: ArgumentNotFound)
member.returns: The argument with default value updated

member.name: M:UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.Locations.IArgumentService.RenameArgument(UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.Locations.ArgumentInfo,System.String)
member.summary: Renames an argument
member.param: New name for the argument. Must be a valid identifier and not duplicate an existing argument name.
member.exception:
Thrown when:
The new name is invalid or empty (error code: InvalidName)
The new name already exists (error code: DuplicateName)

Thrown when:
Unable to resolve the designer (error code: NoActiveDesigner)
Argument not found in the workflow (error code: ArgumentNotFound)
member.returns: The renamed argument

member.name: M:UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.Locations.IArgumentService.CreateArgument(UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.Locations.CreateArgumentArgs)
member.summary: Creates a new argument in the workflow.
member.param: Arguments describing the argument to create. See for details.
member.exception:
Thrown when:
Expression is provided for Out or InOut argument (error code: OnlyInArgumentsSupported)
An argument with the same name already exists (error code: DuplicateName)

Thrown when:
No active designer available (error code: NoActiveDesigner)
Argument creation fails (error code: UnknownError)
member.returns: The newly created argument

remarks.para: The property follows the same format as : string literals must be quoted, and simple values are converted to literals while complex expressions remain as VB/C# expressions.
Duplicate Names: This method will throw an exception if an argument with the same name already exists. Use to pre-validate the name before calling this method.

member.name: M:UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.Locations.IArgumentService.RemoveArgument(UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.Locations.ArgumentInfo)
member.summary: Removes an argument from the workflow.
member.exception:
Thrown when:
Unable to resolve the designer (error code: NoActiveDesigner)
Argument not found in the workflow (error code: ArgumentNotFound)
member.returns: Always returns true on successful removal. Failures are reported via exceptions.

remarks.para: The boolean return value always returns true on success for compatibility with existing implementations. All error conditions are reported through exceptions with error codes rather than returning false.

member.name: M:UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.Locations.IArgumentService.SetIsRequired(UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.Locations.ArgumentInfo,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Sets whether an argument is required.
member.param: True to mark the argument as required, false otherwise
member.exception:
Thrown when:
Unable to resolve the designer (error code: NoActiveDesigner)
Argument with the given name does not exist (error code: ArgumentNotFound)
member.returns: The argument with IsRequired updated

remarks.para: Required arguments must be provided with a value when invoking a workflow. This is enforced at runtime.
If the argument's required state is already set to the requested value, this method returns immediately without making changes.

member.name: T:UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.Locations.ArgumentServiceErrors
member.summary: Error codes for exceptions.

remarks.para: All methods include error codes in thrown exceptions. Access the error code via exception.Data["ErrorCode"] or exception.Data[ErrorConstants.ErrorCode].

item.description: : Input validation failures (invalid names, wrong direction combinations, etc.)
: Operational failures (no designer, argument not found, etc.)

member.summary: Executes a wizard and returns the resulted activity.

member.name: P:UiPath.Studio.Activities.Api.Wizards.WizardBase.RunWizardWithContainerAsync
member.summary: Executes a wizard in realtime, allowing it to directly manipulate the workflow it is called on. Takes precedence over , if both are implemented then only this one will be run. The context can be used to retrieve an initially empty target container, part of the workflow, which it can manipulate freely in real time. The return value indicates whether or not the provided container should be kept as part of the workflow. In case the container should be discarded, any operations done on the workflow outside of the container should be cleaned up by the wizard.
